project.name: phonebot-challenge
project.description: Post-processing pipeline that extracts caller information (first name, last name, email, phone number) from German AI phone bot audio recordings using Deepgram Nova-3 transcription and Claude Sonnet extraction orchestrated via LangGraph state machines. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template listing required environment variables (Deepgram and Anthropic API keys, Deepgram concurrency, Phoenix project name) for users to copy into a local .env file.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Git ignore patterns for Python build artifacts, virtualenvs, .env secrets, IDE folders, the outputs directory, and Phoenix trace storage.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project documentation covering the technical challenge, full solution architecture, V1/V2 pipeline diagrams, component rationale, accuracy results, GEPA optimization workflow, observability, and tradeoffs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Mermaid diagrams to visualize the pipeline topology and state machines for V1/V2 extraction flows.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python package manifest declaring Python 3.13+ requirement, runtime dependencies (LangGraph, LangChain-Anthropic, Deepgram, Phoenix, GEPA, ChromaDB, rapidfuzz), pytest settings, and hatchling build config for the src/phonebot package.

nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: Primary CLI entry point that parses arguments, wires optional modules (postprocess, few-shot, escalation, observability), runs the extraction pipeline orchestrator, computes metrics, and emits final submission payloads (results.json, scores.json, comparison.json) when --final is set.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses argparse.BooleanOptionalAction for module toggle flags and defers heavy imports until after dotenv and tracing are initialized (RESEARCH Pitfall 2).

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Constructs the argparse CLI with flags for model, recordings dir, prompt version, pipeline variant (v1/v2), actor-critic iterations, --final submission mode, and module toggles.

nodes.name: build_final_results_payload
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical outputs/results.json payload for the --final submission, including model name, prompt version, per-recording results with flagged fields, duration, and timestamp.

nodes.name: build_scores_payload
nodes.summary: Builds the outputs/scores.json payload containing per-field and overall accuracy metrics tagged with model and prompt version.

nodes.name: build_comparison_payload
nodes.summary: Builds the outputs/comparison.json payload containing v1 vs v2 per-field accuracy delta and a confidence distribution (high/low/no-confidence counts) derived from CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async orchestration entry point that initializes Phoenix tracing, loads the prompt version, runs the extraction pipeline, auto-evaluates against ground truth, fires observability hooks (alerts, history, errors, regression, latency), and writes submission outputs in --final mode.

nodes.name: optimize.py
nodes.summary: GEPA prompt optimization CLI that runs guided evolutionary search over a 5-slot prompt candidate (system_prompt + 4 field descriptions), evaluates on a fixed train/val split, and writes an optimized prompt JSON plus an optimization report with per-field deltas.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the GEPAAdapter protocol with a custom PhonebotAdapter class that runs the extraction pipeline in-process and uses weighted per-field accuracy (inverse of baseline accuracy) to focus the optimizer on weak fields.

nodes.name: compute_field_weights
nodes.summary: Computes normalized inverse-accuracy field weights with a floor so that GEPA focuses budget on weaker fields while still detecting regressions in high-accuracy fields.

nodes.name: build_seed_candidate
nodes.summary: Reads a prompt JSON file and flattens it into a GEPA seed candidate dict with five slots: system_prompt plus the four field descriptions.

nodes.name: save_optimized_prompt
nodes.summary: Serializes a GEPA candidate dict back into a prompt JSON file with system_prompt and fields sub-keys.

nodes.name: make_train_val_split
nodes.summary: Deterministic fixed-seed random split of recording IDs into train and validation sets for reproducible GEPA runs.

nodes.name: build_dataset
nodes.summary: Builds a GEPA dataset from recording IDs by loading cached transcripts and wrapping each as an input/context/answer dict.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs the full GEPA optimization: computes baseline accuracy, runs gepa.optimize with the custom PhonebotAdapter and an Opus reflection LM, evaluates the optimized prompt on validation, and writes the optimization report with per-field deltas.

nodes.name: RecordingTrajectory
nodes.summary: Simple data container storing per-recording GEPA trajectory information (transcript, predicted extraction, ground truth, failures, score, ASI feedback) used by the reflective dataset builder.

nodes.name: PhonebotAdapter
nodes.summary: Custom GEPAAdapter implementation that runs the extraction pipeline in-process for each candidate, scores with weighted per-field accuracy, and formats per-recording trajectories into ASI feedback for GEPA's reflection LM.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets propose_new_texts = None to opt into GEPA's default reflection-LM-based proposer rather than custom text generation.

nodes.name: compare.py
nodes.summary: A/B comparison CLI that reads all per-model result files from outputs/results_*.json, computes accuracy and latency metrics, prints Rich tables highlighting per-field winners and per-recording diffs, and writes outputs/comparison.json.

nodes.name: load_result_files
nodes.summary: Loads all per-model result JSON payloads from outputs/results_*.json, attaches a descriptive label derived from pipeline/prompt version metadata, and returns them sorted.

nodes.name: build_comparison
nodes.summary: Computes per-model metrics, indexes per-recording extractions, collects fields where any two models disagree (with ground truth), and determines the overall-accuracy winner or tie.

nodes.name: print_comparison
nodes.summary: Renders the comparison as two Rich tables per the UI-SPEC contract: per-field accuracy with best values highlighted, per-recording differences against ground truth, and a winner/tie summary line.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that loads result files, requires at least two to compare, runs build_comparison and print_comparison, then writes outputs/comparison.json.

nodes.name: ground_truth.json
nodes.summary: Gold-standard ground truth file mapping each of 30 call recording IDs to expected first_name, last_name, email, and phone_number values used to score the extraction pipeline.

nodes.name: german_names.json
nodes.summary: Curated German and international first/last name dictionary sourced from GfdS, Destatis, and international name frequency data; used by rapidfuzz name grounding to confidence-penalize extracted names outside the dictionary.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker docstring identifying phonebot as the audio entity extraction package root.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers used across phonebot modules: cached git commit lookup, JSON file writer with parent directory creation, and E.164 phone normalization with German default region.

nodes.name: get_git_commit
nodes.summary: Returns the current short git commit hash via subprocess with a 5-second timeout, cached with lru_cache, and returns None on any failure.

nodes.name: normalize_phone_e164
nodes.summary: Normalizes a phone number to E.164 format using the phonenumbers library with DE as default region; returns original on parse failure and None on None input.

nodes.name: prompts/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Prompt management module that loads externalized JSON prompts and dynamically constructs a Pydantic CallerInfo model at runtime — enabling hot-swappable prompts for GEPA optimization without code changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pydantic.create_model() to synthesize a Pydantic class from the prompt JSON's fields dict, with __doc__ set to the system_prompt so LangChain's with_structured_output uses it as the system message.

nodes.name: build_caller_info_model
nodes.summary: Factory that reads a prompt JSON file and dynamically constructs a CallerInfo Pydantic class with Optional[str] fields derived from the JSON's fields dict, plus a required confidence dict field.

nodes.name: export_v1_prompt
nodes.summary: Exports the inline CallerInfo class's docstring and field descriptions to a prompt JSON file suitable for use as the GEPA seed candidate and baseline.

nodes.name: critic_prompt.json
nodes.summary: Externalized system prompt for the V2 actor-critic pipeline's critic LLM, instructing strict per-field verification against the transcript with evidence quotes and field-specific rules for names, email, and phone.

nodes.name: extraction_v1.json
nodes.summary: Baseline extraction prompt with concise system instructions and four field descriptions covering German email components (Punkt/at/minus), phone digit reconstruction, and letter-by-letter spelling.

nodes.name: extraction_v2.json
nodes.summary: GEPA-optimized extraction prompt for the V1 pipeline with an expanded system message covering name/email cross-referencing, digit-letter confusion corrections, umlaut transliteration, and phone digit counting rules.

nodes.name: extraction_v2_ac.json
nodes.summary: GEPA-optimized extraction prompt variant tuned for the V2 actor-critic pipeline, emphasizing common-provider email defaults and cautious name preservation since the critic will catch errors downstream.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the models package that re-exports CallerInfo and the chat model registry helpers as the stable public surface of phonebot.models.

nodes.name: caller_info.py
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical CallerInfo Pydantic model with four extraction fields (first_name, last_name, email, phone_number) plus a confidence dict, whose Field descriptions double as the LLM extraction prompt for German phone bot transcripts.
nodes.languageNotes: Field description strings function as the LLM's extraction instructions — the Pydantic schema IS the prompt.

nodes.name: CallerInfo
nodes.summary: Pydantic model representing an extracted caller record; Field descriptions encode detailed instructions for Deepgram transcript quirks (phonetic approximations, spoken email tokens, spaced digits).

nodes.name: model_registry.py
nodes.summary: Provider-agnostic LangChain chat model factory that routes 'claude-*' to ChatAnthropic and 'ollama:<name>' to ChatOllama, plus a filesystem-safe alias helper for run artifacts.

nodes.name: get_model
nodes.summary: Returns a LangChain chat model instance based on a prefix convention (claude-* or ollama:<model>), failing fast with actionable errors on missing API keys or unrecognized names.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the extraction pipeline subpackage containing the V1 single-pass and V2 actor-critic LangGraph implementations.

nodes.name: classifier.py
nodes.summary: Rule-based transcript difficulty classifier that scores signals (length, speakers, spelling markers, low-confidence words) to route recordings to EASY/MEDIUM/HARD tiers with zero LLM cost.

nodes.name: DifficultyTier
nodes.summary: String enum of difficulty tiers (EASY, MEDIUM, HARD) used to select between V1 and V2 extraction pipelines.

nodes.name: DifficultySignals
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling individual transcript signals (length, speakers, email/phone markers, spelling, low-confidence words) that feed the difficulty scoring function.

nodes.name: ClassificationResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass returned from classify_transcript carrying the tier, score, signals, and the recommended pipeline version, critic iteration count, and few-shot flag.

nodes.name: classify_transcript
nodes.summary: Loads a cached transcript and computes a difficulty score from multiple heuristic signals, mapping it onto an EASY/MEDIUM/HARD tier with the corresponding pipeline routing recommendation.

nodes.name: classify_batch
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that classifies a list of recording IDs and returns a dict keyed by recording ID.

nodes.name: escalation.py
nodes.summary: Human-in-the-loop escalation layer that flags low-confidence extractions (by average confidence, flagged field count, or invalid contacts) and writes them to a JSON queue for manual review.

nodes.name: EscalationItem
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a recording escalated for review, including the extracted info, reason, flagged fields, transcript excerpt, and overall confidence.

nodes.name: check_escalation
nodes.summary: Evaluates an extraction against escalation rules (low overall confidence, too many flagged fields, invalid phone or email) and returns an EscalationItem when any trigger fires.

nodes.name: write_escalation_queue
nodes.summary: Serializes a list of EscalationItems to a JSON file, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: extract.py
nodes.summary: V1 single-pass LangGraph extraction pipeline (transcribe → extract → validate) with up to two retries on Pydantic failure and a hot-swappable CallerInfo model for GEPA prompt optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LangGraph StateGraph with conditional edges for retry routing; _CALLER_INFO_MODEL module-level singleton enables prompt hot-swapping without rebuilding the compiled graph.

nodes.name: transcribe_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that loads the cached transcript JSON for the recording and returns its plain text into pipeline state.

nodes.name: build_pipeline
nodes.summary: Constructs the V1 LangGraph StateGraph with transcribe, extract, and validate nodes plus a conditional retry edge back to extract on validation failure.

nodes.name: run_pipeline
nodes.summary: Async batch runner for the V1 pipeline that delegates to run_pipeline_concurrent with base initial-state and result-builder factories and an optional override for few-shot injection.

nodes.name: extract_v2.py
nodes.summary: V2 actor-critic LangGraph extraction pipeline that adds a critic LLM loop after Pydantic validation, with per-field verdicts and an actor-refine step that deliberately anchors to correct fields while fixing only the ones marked 'needs_fix'.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Literal types and Pydantic structured output for per-field critic verdicts; distinguishes retry (discard output to avoid anchoring) from refine (preserve correct fields).

nodes.name: CriticVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single critic verdict on one extracted field, with status, issue description, and transcript evidence quote.

nodes.name: CriticOutput
nodes.summary: Pydantic model bundling the critic's overall_approved flag, per-field verdicts list, and summary_feedback paragraph returned via LLM structured output.

nodes.name: ActorCriticState
nodes.summary: TypedDict extending BasePipelineState with actor-critic fields (ac_iteration, ac_max_iterations, critic_approved, critic_feedback, ac_history) for the V2 LangGraph state schema.

nodes.name: critic_evaluate_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that invokes the critic LLM with the transcript and current extraction (confidence stripped), parses a CriticOutput, and appends an entry to ac_history for observability.

nodes.name: actor_refine_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that re-prompts the actor LLM with the previous extraction, critic feedback, and field verdicts, explicitly instructing it to fix only 'needs_fix' fields while preserving correct ones.

nodes.name: build_actor_critic_pipeline
nodes.summary: Builds the V2 StateGraph topology with transcribe, actor_extract, pydantic_validate, critic_evaluate, actor_refine, and pydantic_validate_refined nodes wired by three conditional routing functions.

nodes.name: run_pipeline_v2
nodes.summary: Async batch runner for the V2 actor-critic pipeline that extends base initial state and result builder with actor-critic observability fields (ac_iterations_used, critic_approved, ac_history).

nodes.name: orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Top-level LangGraph StateGraph that composes the full pipeline (classify → [few-shot retrieve] → extract V1 or V2 → [postprocess] → [escalation]) per recording, with config flags toggling each optional stage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-level singleton with double-checked locking for the non-serializable ChromaDB example store; delegates to inner compiled V1/V2 graphs rather than fusing them into a single megagraph.

nodes.name: OrchestratorState
nodes.summary: TypedDict for the orchestrator graph state carrying inputs, feature flags, classification results, few-shot prefix, extraction outputs, and escalation results across all stages.

nodes.name: classify_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the transcript difficulty classifier and eagerly loads the transcript text so downstream nodes can reuse it without re-reading the cache.

nodes.name: extract_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that dispatches to the compiled V1 or V2 inner graph based on pipeline_version, maps orchestrator state into inner state (including few-shot prefix and actor-critic config), and returns caller_info plus flagged fields.

nodes.name: postprocess_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that invokes the postprocess stage (normalization + knowledge grounding) and recomputes flagged fields from the adjusted confidence scores.

nodes.name: escalation_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that invokes check_escalation with the current caller info, flagged fields, transcript excerpt, and contact validation results.

nodes.name: build_orchestrator_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles the orchestrator StateGraph with classify, few_shot_retrieve, extract, postprocess, and escalation nodes wired by three conditional routing functions.

nodes.name: run_orchestrator_pipeline
nodes.summary: Async public entry point that drives the orchestrator graph over a batch of recordings, aggregates escalation items, and writes the escalation queue after all per-item graphs complete.

nodes.name: shared.py
nodes.summary: Shared pipeline infrastructure used by V1 and V2 — Pydantic validation helper, LLM extraction helper, BasePipelineState TypedDict, PipelineVersion enum, and the concurrent batch runner with OpenTelemetry attribute tagging and per-recording timeouts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses openinference.using_attributes context manager to tag each Phoenix trace with recording_id, model, and prompt_version metadata; asyncio.Semaphore bounds concurrency from EXTRACT_CONCURRENCY env var.

nodes.name: PipelineVersion
nodes.summary: String enum identifying the extraction pipeline version (V1 or V2), used for routing and tracing.

nodes.name: BasePipelineState
nodes.summary: TypedDict defining the base LangGraph state contract shared by V1 and V2 pipelines: recording_id, transcript_text, caller_info, retry_count, validation_errors, few_shot_prefix.

nodes.name: validate_caller_info
nodes.summary: Shared validation implementation that runs Pydantic validation on the extracted caller_info, clearing errors on success or incrementing retry_count with error messages on failure.

nodes.name: extract_caller_info
nodes.summary: Shared LLM extraction implementation that prepends few_shot_prefix if present, injects previous validation errors on retry (but not the failed output itself), and invokes the structured_output model.

nodes.name: run_pipeline_concurrent
nodes.summary: Shared async batch runner that invokes a compiled LangGraph over recording IDs with semaphore-bounded concurrency, per-recording timeouts, OpenTelemetry metadata, and graceful error handling that falls back to an empty caller_info result.

nodes.name: transcribe.py
nodes.summary: Async batch Deepgram Nova-3 transcription with JSON caching plus analysis helpers that inspect raw transcripts against ground truth to generate a smart_format behavior report documenting German phone/email tokenization quirks.
nodes.languageNotes: load_dotenv must run before the deepgram import because the SDK evaluates DEEPGRAM_API_KEY at import time as a default parameter.

nodes.name: transcribe_all
nodes.summary: Async function that transcribes all WAV files in the recordings directory with semaphore-bounded concurrency, skipping any recording with an existing cache file.

nodes.name: get_transcript_text
nodes.summary: Extracts the plain transcript string from a cached Deepgram JSON response.

nodes.name: get_words
nodes.summary: Extracts the word-level data array (with timing, confidence, and speaker) from a cached Deepgram JSON response.

nodes.name: generate_smart_format_report
nodes.summary: Analyzes sampled transcripts against ground truth to document Deepgram smart_format behavior for German phone numbers, emails, foreign names, and diarization, writing a markdown report to docs/.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker describing the stages subpackage that provides rule-based normalization, knowledge grounding, and contact validation post-extraction.

nodes.name: postprocess.py
nodes.summary: Post-processing stage that applies rule-based normalization (phone E.164, email lowercasing, Unicode NFC names), knowledge grounding via name lookup, contact pattern validation, and name↔email cross-reference checks to adjust confidence scores.

nodes.name: PostprocessResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling the normalized CallerInfo, knowledge grounding results, contact validation, cross-reference check, and a log of applied normalizations.

nodes.name: postprocess
nodes.summary: Runs the full post-processing pipeline: normalizes phone, email, and names; validates contacts; grounds names against the dictionary; cross-references name to email; and reconciles the final confidence dict.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the knowledge grounding layer providing name lookup, contact pattern validation, and few-shot example retrieval.

nodes.name: contact_patterns.py
nodes.summary: Rule-based contact validator that checks phone numbers via the phonenumbers library (E.164, number type) and emails via regex plus a curated known-domains set, then applies confidence penalties for invalid or unknown values.

nodes.name: validate_phone
nodes.summary: Validates a phone number against German patterns using phonenumbers, returning validity, number type (mobile/landline/voip), E.164 format, and any parsing issues.

nodes.name: validate_email
nodes.summary: Validates an email address format via regex and checks whether its domain appears in the curated KNOWN_DOMAINS set, returning structured issues for downstream confidence adjustment.

nodes.name: adjust_confidence
nodes.summary: Applies confidence penalties to phone and email fields when contact pattern validation fails or the domain is unknown, returning an adjusted confidence dict.

nodes.name: example_store.py
nodes.summary: ChromaDB-backed few-shot example store that indexes transcript-to-ground-truth pairs and retrieves the most similar past recordings at extraction time, formatting them as few-shot prompt prefixes for hard foreign-name cases.

nodes.name: ExampleStore
nodes.summary: Persistent ChromaDB wrapper exposing index_ground_truth, retrieve, and format_few_shot_prompt operations over a cosine-distance transcript collection for RAG-style example retrieval.

nodes.name: name_lookup.py
nodes.summary: Fuzzy name grounding layer that matches extracted first/last names against a curated German name dictionary via rapidfuzz and penalizes confidence for ungrounded names (does not overwrite extractions).

nodes.name: lookup_name
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-matches a name against a list of known names using rapidfuzz.fuzz.ratio with a configurable threshold, returning the top candidates with scores and grounded flags.

nodes.name: ground_name
nodes.summary: Checks an extracted name against the appropriate (first/last) name list, applying an UNGROUNDED_PENALTY to confidence when no match clears the threshold.

nodes.name: ground_caller_info
nodes.summary: Applies ground_name to both first_name and last_name fields in a CallerInfo dict and returns a per-field grounding result dict.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the evaluation module with a short docstring describing its role as the home for metrics and ground-truth comparison helpers.

nodes.name: benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Difficulty-tiered benchmark evaluation that segments recordings by name origin, email complexity, and phone format to expose where extraction accuracy drops.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass TierResult plus lambda classifiers to parameterize per-tier metric computation.

nodes.name: error_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Categorizes extraction failures into specific error types (phonetic mismatch, email assembly, phone digit error, hallucination, omission) using string distance and pattern matching, without LLM calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Field classifier registry (_FIELD_CLASSIFIERS) acts as a dispatch table — extend by adding entries rather than modifying the main loop.

nodes.name: history.py
nodes.summary: Appends per-run metrics records to a JSONL history file with timestamp, model, prompt version, pipeline, and git commit to enable trend analysis across runs.

nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Core accuracy metrics engine that normalizes field values and compares extraction results against ground truth, producing per-field accuracy, overall accuracy, and per-recording breakdowns.
nodes.languageNotes: FIELDS is re-exported from phonebot.models.caller_info.EXTRACTION_FIELDS for backward compatibility — extending FIELDS is how new entity types enter evaluation.

nodes.name: regression.py
nodes.summary: Regression testing that snapshots a baseline of metrics and flags field-level and per-recording accuracy drops exceeding a configurable tolerance.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Bootstraps Arize Phoenix tracing via init_tracing() and shutdown_tracing(), wiring Phoenix OTEL auto-instrumentation while handling idempotent restart and port-in-use scenarios.
nodes.languageNotes: Stores tracer provider at module scope so shutdown_tracing() can call force_flush() before exit.

nodes.name: alerts.py
nodes.summary: Threshold-based quality alerting that checks accuracy, cost, latency, and escalation rate after every pipeline run and prints Rich warnings plus structlog events.

nodes.name: cost_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Accumulates per-invocation LLM token usage with model-specific pricing tables and reports per-recording and per-node cost breakdowns.
nodes.languageNotes: PRICING dict is the single source of truth — add new models by inserting entries, with ollama sentinel for free local models.

nodes.name: latency.py
nodes.summary: SLA-budget latency monitor that records per-node durations via a context manager and flags violations against configurable budgets, reporting averages, p95, and violations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses contextlib.contextmanager with time.monotonic for drift-free timing.

nodes.name: logging.py
nodes.summary: Structured (JSON) logging configuration via structlog plus convenience helpers for common pipeline events (extraction start/complete, validation failure, escalation, retries).

nodes.name: prompt_registry.py
nodes.summary: Prompt version registry that hashes prompt content (SHA256) and associates each hash with timestamp, git commit, and accuracy metrics for exact reproducibility.

nodes.name: TierResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding one tier's evaluation outcome: name, value, matching recording IDs, per-field accuracy, overall accuracy, and count.

nodes.name: compute_tiered_benchmarks
nodes.summary: Computes extraction accuracy broken down by three tier dimensions — name origin, email complexity, and phone format — returning a dict of TierResult lists.

nodes.name: _compute_tier
nodes.summary: Groups results by a classification function and computes per-group metrics, returning one TierResult per distinct classification value.

nodes.name: print_benchmark_report
nodes.summary: Renders tier benchmark results as Rich tables with columns for each extraction field plus overall accuracy.

nodes.name: save_benchmark_report
nodes.summary: Serializes tier benchmark results to JSON with rounded accuracy values and per-tier recording ID lists.

nodes.name: ErrorType
nodes.summary: String Enum of extraction error categories covering name phonetic/spelling issues, email domain/assembly errors, phone digit errors, hallucination, omission, and null mismatch.

nodes.name: ErrorRecord
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a single classified extraction error with recording id, field, error type, predicted and expected values, and a detail string.

nodes.name: _classify_name_error
nodes.summary: Classifies a name field discrepancy into spelling variant, phonetic mismatch, omission, null mismatch, or hallucination based on Levenshtein similarity and first-letter heuristics.

nodes.name: _classify_email_error
nodes.summary: Classifies email errors by comparing domain exactly and local-part via Levenshtein similarity, distinguishing assembly errors from outright hallucinations.

nodes.name: _classify_phone_error
nodes.summary: Classifies phone errors by counting digit length mismatches and positional digit differences, flagging hallucinations when too many digits diverge.

nodes.name: analyze_errors
nodes.summary: Iterates over extraction results and ground truth, normalizing and comparing each field, and dispatching mismatches through the per-field classifier registry.

nodes.name: error_distribution
nodes.summary: Counts errors by error type and returns a dict sorted by descending count.

nodes.name: print_error_analysis
nodes.summary: Prints overall error distribution, per-field breakdowns, and a detailed enumeration of each error as Rich tables and styled lines.

nodes.name: save_error_analysis
nodes.summary: Serializes error analysis (totals, distribution, per-field breakdown, and individual errors) to JSON.

nodes.name: record_run
nodes.summary: Appends a metrics record to the JSONL history file, capturing timestamp, model, prompt version, pipeline version, per-field/overall accuracy, git commit, and optional extras.

nodes.name: load_history
nodes.summary: Loads all JSONL history records from disk and returns them as a list, gracefully handling a missing file.

nodes.name: print_history
nodes.summary: Renders the last N runs as a Rich table showing timestamp, model, pipeline, prompt version, per-field accuracy, overall accuracy, and git commit.

nodes.name: matches_field
nodes.summary: Compares a predicted value against ground truth for a single field, handling null, scalar, and list ground truth forms with appropriate normalization.

nodes.name: compute_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes per-field accuracy, overall accuracy, and per-recording breakdown for a list of extraction results against ground truth, handling both dict and model_dump-compatible CallerInfo inputs.

nodes.name: save_baseline
nodes.summary: Snapshots current metrics (per-field, overall, per-recording, plus model/prompt/pipeline context) as the regression baseline JSON file.

nodes.name: check_regression
nodes.summary: Loads the baseline, computes per-field deltas, flags fields that regressed beyond tolerance, and reports per-recording regressions where a previously-correct field is now wrong.

nodes.name: print_regression_report
nodes.summary: Prints accuracy deltas vs baseline as a Rich table with color-coded pass/fail/regression status and lists recording-level regressions.

nodes.name: init_tracing
nodes.summary: Starts the Phoenix server idempotently (detecting an existing session or port-in-use), configures persistent working dir, and registers the Phoenix OTEL tracer provider with auto-instrumentation.

nodes.name: shutdown_tracing
nodes.summary: Flushes pending spans on the stored tracer provider before process exit, safely no-ops when tracing was never initialized.

nodes.name: AlertThresholds
nodes.summary: Dataclass of configurable alert thresholds for overall/per-field accuracy, cost per recording, latency per recording, and escalation rate.

nodes.name: Alert
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a triggered alert with severity, category, message, actual value, and threshold.

nodes.name: check_alerts
nodes.summary: Runs all alert checks against pipeline run metrics, cost, latency, and escalation counts, returning a list of triggered Alert objects.

nodes.name: print_alerts
nodes.summary: Prints triggered alerts as color-coded Rich warnings and emits structlog warning events for each alert.

nodes.name: TokenRecord
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing a single LLM invocation's token counts, latency, node, and model, with a cached cost_usd property computed from the pricing table.

nodes.name: CostTracker
nodes.summary: Accumulator class that records per-invocation token usage and exposes totals, grouping by recording or pipeline node, with JSON serialization and a Rich summary printer.

nodes.name: LatencyRecord
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a single node execution timing with recording id, node, duration, optional budget, and derived within_budget and overage_pct properties.

nodes.name: LatencyMonitor
nodes.summary: Records per-node latencies via a context manager, computes averages and p95 per node, tracks SLA violations, and renders a Rich summary highlighting violations.

nodes.name: configure_logging
nodes.summary: Configures structlog with either JSON or console renderers plus stdlib integration, routing output to stderr or a log file, and is idempotent via a module flag.

nodes.name: get_logger
nodes.summary: Returns a bound structlog logger, auto-configuring logging from env vars on first call and binding any initial context.

nodes.name: register_prompt
nodes.summary: Registers a prompt file by hashing its content, recording version, timestamp, git commit, and a slot for accuracy to be filled later.

nodes.name: update_accuracy
nodes.summary: Updates a registered prompt's accuracy metrics by content hash, connecting prompt versions to the accuracy they produced.

nodes.name: print_prompt_history
nodes.summary: Prints the prompt version registry as a Rich table showing hash, version, timestamp, git commit, and overall accuracy for each registered prompt.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes the tests directory a Python package for pytest discovery.

nodes.name: test_alerts.py
nodes.summary: Tests the quality alerting system covering accuracy, cost, latency, escalation, and threshold-based alert triggers.

nodes.name: TestAlerts
nodes.summary: Test suite verifying the alert engine's behavior across accuracy, field-level, cost, latency, escalation, and custom-threshold scenarios.

nodes.name: test_benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Tests the difficulty-tiered benchmark classifier that splits recordings by name origin, email complexity, and phone format.

nodes.name: TestEmailClassification
nodes.summary: Verifies email tier classification (simple, hyphenated, foreign_domain, missing).

nodes.name: TestPhoneClassification
nodes.summary: Verifies phone number classification into mobile, landline, and missing tiers.

nodes.name: TestTierSets
nodes.summary: Checks that the hardcoded German and international recording ID sets partition the 30 test recordings.

nodes.name: TestTieredBenchmarks
nodes.summary: Integration test for compute_tiered_benchmarks ensuring tiers group correctly and accuracy is computed per tier.

nodes.name: test_classifier.py
nodes.summary: Tests the transcript difficulty classifier that detects spelling events, email complexity, speaker counts, and routes recordings to the appropriate pipeline.

nodes.name: TestSpellingDetection
nodes.summary: Verifies detection of letter-by-letter spelling sequences (e.g., M A T T H I A S) in transcripts.

nodes.name: TestEmailComplexity
nodes.summary: Verifies scoring of email complexity signals: simple, hyphenated, digits, and foreign domains.

nodes.name: TestSpeakerCount
nodes.summary: Validates speaker diarization counting from Deepgram word-level data.

nodes.name: TestClassificationResult
nodes.summary: Exercises the ClassificationResult dataclass for easy vs. hard difficulty tiers with v1/v2 pipeline routing.

nodes.name: test_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI smoke tests for run.py covering argument parsing, help output, --final flag, prompt version selection, and final results payload builders.

nodes.name: test_compare.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for compare.py that exercise multi-model comparison logic including per-field accuracy, winner declaration, ties, and N-model support.

nodes.name: test_cost_tracker.py
nodes.summary: Tests the cost tracker that computes per-recording and per-node LLM token costs for Claude and free Ollama models.

nodes.name: TestTokenRecord
nodes.summary: Verifies per-invocation cost computation for Claude pricing and zero-cost Ollama models.

nodes.name: TestPricingResolution
nodes.summary: Validates _resolve_pricing lookup for known models, ollama prefix routing, and unknown-model fallback.

nodes.name: TestCostTracker
nodes.summary: Exercises CostTracker aggregation APIs: record, totals, cost_by_recording, cost_by_node, to_dict, and metadata ingestion.

nodes.name: test_error_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Tests the error analysis pipeline that classifies extraction failures into omission, spelling variant, email domain, assembly, and phone digit error categories.

nodes.name: TestErrorClassification
nodes.summary: Validates error type classification for each error category supported by analyze_errors.

nodes.name: TestErrorDistribution
nodes.summary: Verifies error_distribution and error_distribution_by_field aggregate error counts correctly.

nodes.name: test_escalation.py
nodes.summary: Tests the human-in-the-loop escalation path that flags low-confidence extractions and persists an escalation queue.

nodes.name: TestEscalationCheck
nodes.summary: Covers check_escalation triggers for low confidence, too many flagged fields, and invalid phone numbers.

nodes.name: TestEscalationQueue
nodes.summary: Verifies escalation queue persistence: write/load roundtrip and empty-queue skip behavior.

nodes.name: test_evaluation_metrics.py
nodes.summary: Tests the core evaluation metrics module covering phone/text normalization, field matching, ground-truth loading, and metric computation.

nodes.name: TestNormalizePhone
nodes.summary: Verifies phone normalization to E.164 format for German numbers with and without spaces.

nodes.name: TestNormalizeText
nodes.summary: Covers text normalization including lowercase, whitespace stripping, German umlauts, and eszett folding.

nodes.name: TestMatchesField
nodes.summary: Validates matches_field logic across case-insensitive matching, None handling, multi-value ground truth, and field-specific normalization.

nodes.name: TestLoadGroundTruth
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the 30-recording ground truth JSON schema.

nodes.name: TestComputeMetrics
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for compute_metrics: zero accuracy, perfect accuracy, per-recording breakdown, and CallerInfo object ingestion.

nodes.name: test_extract.py
nodes.summary: Tests the LangGraph v1 extraction pipeline covering graph topology, CallerInfo field descriptions, retry loop, validation node, and flagged field computation.

nodes.name: test_extract_v2.py
nodes.summary: Tests the Actor-Critic v2 LangGraph pipeline covering node topology, routing functions, critic schemas, iteration cap, and output format compatibility with v1.

nodes.name: test_history.py
nodes.summary: Tests the metrics history tracking module that appends run records to a JSONL file for longitudinal comparison.

nodes.name: TestMetricsHistory
nodes.summary: Verifies record_run and load_history round-trip, extra metadata capture, and append-only semantics.

nodes.name: test_knowledge.py
nodes.summary: Tests the knowledge grounding layer: name lookup against corpora, contact pattern validation, and confidence adjustment from validation signals.

nodes.name: TestNameLookup
nodes.summary: Exercises fuzzy name lookup against first/last name corpora and the ground_caller_info confidence adjustment helper.

nodes.name: TestContactPatterns
nodes.summary: Validates phone (E.164, number_type) and email (format, known_domain) pattern checks plus adjust_confidence penalty logic.

nodes.name: test_latency.py
nodes.summary: Tests the latency monitor observability module covering per-node recording, budget violations, p95 percentiles, and context-manager tracking.

nodes.name: TestLatencyRecord
nodes.summary: Verifies LatencyRecord budget-comparison logic and overage percentage calculation.

nodes.name: TestLatencyMonitor
nodes.summary: Tests LatencyMonitor context-manager tracking, violations detection, avg/p95 aggregates, and default budget presence.

nodes.name: test_model_registry.py
nodes.summary: Tests model registry routing for claude-* to ChatAnthropic, ollama:* to ChatOllama, error handling for unknown prefixes, and model_alias filename sanitization.

nodes.name: test_observability.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for init_tracing and shutdown_tracing with mocked Phoenix server covering register() kwargs, launch_app persistence, active-session reuse, and force_flush on shutdown.

nodes.name: test_optimize.py
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for optimize.py GEPA plumbing covering field weight computation, seed candidate building, prompt save round-trip, and deterministic train/val splits.

nodes.name: test_prompt_registry.py
nodes.summary: Tests the prompt version registry that hashes prompt content, registers versions, updates accuracy, and deduplicates identical prompts.

nodes.name: TestPromptRegistry
nodes.summary: Exercises prompt registry CRUD: hash determinism, register, update_accuracy, history retrieval, and content-hash deduplication.

nodes.name: test_prompts.py
nodes.summary: Tests the externalized prompt loader and build_caller_info_model factory that dynamically constructs a Pydantic CallerInfo model from JSON for GEPA optimization.

nodes.name: test_regression.py
nodes.summary: Tests the regression detection suite covering baseline save/load, tolerance thresholds, per-field regressions, per-recording regressions, and delta computation.

nodes.name: TestRegression
nodes.summary: Covers all regression detection scenarios: no baseline, no regression, tolerance-bound, detected regressions, per-recording, and delta reporting.

nodes.name: test_structured_logging.py
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for the structured logging module ensuring configure_logging is idempotent and all convenience log functions execute without errors.

nodes.name: TestStructuredLogging
nodes.summary: Validates get_logger, idempotent configure_logging, and all convenience log_* helpers for pipeline events.

nodes.name: test_transcribe.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Deepgram transcription module with mocked SDK v6 client covering cache skip, API keyword args, cache JSON structure, concurrency dispatch, and env-var configuration.

layers.name: Entry Points & CLI
layers.description: Thin command-line wrappers that drive the pipeline, GEPA prompt optimization, and result-comparison workflows by delegating into the phonebot package modules.

layers.name: Project Configuration & Documentation
layers.description: Project manifest, environment template, ignore rules, and the README that describe how to install, configure, and run the phonebot challenge.

layers.name: Data Models
layers.description: Pydantic schemas for caller information and the LangChain model registry that defines the structured contracts shared across pipeline stages.

layers.name: Prompts & LLM Contracts
layers.description: Externalized JSON prompt templates for v1 extraction, v2 actor-critic extraction, and the critic, plus the loader that exposes them to pipeline nodes.

layers.name: Pipeline Orchestration
layers.description: LangGraph state machines and stage modules that transcribe audio, classify intent, extract caller fields (v1 and actor-critic v2), normalize results, escalate ambiguous cases, and orchestrate the end-to-end run.

layers.name: Knowledge & Grounding
layers.description: Rule-based name lookup, contact pattern matchers, and the ChromaDB-backed example store used to ground LLM extraction in German naming and contact conventions.

layers.name: Evaluation & Metrics
layers.description: Benchmarking, scoring metrics, error analysis, regression checks, and run history persistence used to measure extraction accuracy against ground truth.

layers.name: Observability
layers.description: Phoenix OTel tracing, structured logging, latency tracking, cost accounting, alerting, and prompt registry instrumentation that surrounds every pipeline run.

layers.name: Reference Data & Audio Corpus
layers.description: Ground truth labels, German name knowledge base, raw WAV recordings, and the cached Deepgram transcripts that the pipeline consumes and evaluates against.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest modules that mirror each phonebot source module, covering CLI behavior, pipeline stages, knowledge helpers, evaluation, observability, and prompt loading.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Begin with the README to understand the technical challenge: post-process German AI phone bot recordings and extract four caller fields (first name, last name, email, phone). This document lays out the full architecture — Deepgram Nova-3 transcription feeding into LangGraph V1 (single-pass) and V2 (actor-critic) pipelines, with rule-based post-processing, knowledge grounding, evaluation, and Phoenix observability — and explains the headline 93% accuracy result that the rest of the tour will reverse-engineer through code.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: run.py is the primary CLI that the README's Quick Start invokes. It parses arguments, wires up the optional subsystems (post-processing, few-shot RAG, escalation queue, observability), then hands a list of recordings to the pipeline orchestrator and reports metrics. Notice it is a thin wrapper — all business logic lives inside src/phonebot modules — and pyproject.toml tells you which dependencies (LangGraph, LangChain-Anthropic, Deepgram, Phoenix, GEPA, ChromaDB, rapidfuzz) the wrapper assembles.
tour.languageLesson: Python 'thin entry point' pattern: keeping run.py as a wrapper around importable modules makes the same logic reusable from tests, notebooks, and the GEPA optimizer (Step 11) without spawning subprocesses.

tour.title: LangGraph Orchestrator
tour.description: orchestrator.py is the top-level LangGraph StateGraph that composes the per-recording pipeline: classify difficulty, optionally retrieve few-shot examples, run extract V1 or V2, optionally post-process, and optionally enqueue for human review. Each stage is a graph node with config flags, so the same orchestrator can express the v1-bare baseline through the full v2-with-knowledge configuration described in the README's accuracy table.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph StateGraph: nodes are pure functions that take and return a typed state dict; edges (including conditional edges) wire control flow. This makes the pipeline a directed graph rather than a chain, which is what allows V2's critic→refine loop to fit alongside V1's straight-through path.

tour.title: Transcribe and Route
tour.description: Two stages run before any LLM extraction. transcribe.py wraps the Deepgram Nova-3 SDK with JSON caching so re-running the pipeline is free, and analyses raw transcripts against ground truth to surface which German phone/email patterns smart_format gets right and wrong. classifier.py is a zero-cost rule-based scorer that reads transcript length, speaker turns, spelling markers, and low-confidence words to assign each recording to EASY/MEDIUM/HARD — the tier that the rest of the pipeline uses to decide whether to spend extra LLM budget.

tour.title: V1 Extraction and Caller Schema
tour.description: extract.py is the V1 LangGraph subgraph: a single LLM call against the cached transcript, validated against a Pydantic CallerInfo schema, with up to two retries on validation failure. caller_info.py defines that schema — four extraction fields plus a confidence dict — and crucially the Pydantic Field descriptions double as the LLM extraction prompt, so the schema and the prompt are the same artifact. This is the simplest pipeline that already gets you to the README's V1 baseline accuracy.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic-as-prompt: by treating Field(description=...) as the source of truth for both LLM instructions and structured-output validation, you eliminate the drift between 'what the prompt asks for' and 'what the parser accepts' — a common failure mode in LLM extraction systems.

tour.title: V2 Actor-Critic Loop
tour.description: extract_v2.py upgrades V1 by adding a critic LLM that verifies each extracted field against the transcript and emits per-field verdicts, then loops back to an actor-refine step that anchors correct fields and re-asks for the wrong ones. This is the README's 'actor-critic' pipeline that buys accuracy at the cost of extra latency and tokens. Reading this file alongside extract.py from Step 5 makes it obvious how LangGraph's conditional edges express the refine loop without rewriting the V1 nodes.

tour.title: Externalized Prompts for GEPA
tour.description: prompts/__init__.py loads prompt JSON files at runtime and dynamically constructs the CallerInfo Pydantic model from the loaded field descriptions, so prompts are hot-swappable without code changes. extraction_v1.json is the baseline 5-slot prompt (one system instruction + four field descriptions) covering German conventions like 'Punkt' for dot and letter-by-letter spelling. This split is exactly what enables the GEPA optimizer in Step 11 to evolve prompts as data instead of editing source code.

tour.title: Post-Processing and Knowledge Grounding
tour.description: After extraction, postprocess.py applies deterministic rules (E.164 phone normalization, email lowercasing, Unicode NFC names) and then consults the knowledge layer. name_lookup.py uses rapidfuzz to ground first/last names against a curated German name dictionary, contact_patterns.py validates phones via the phonenumbers library and emails via regex plus a known-domains set, and both adjust confidence rather than overwriting the LLM extraction. example_store.py is the ChromaDB-backed few-shot RAG used for HARD recordings — it indexes past transcript→ground-truth pairs and retrieves the most similar ones as in-context examples.

tour.title: Few-Shot RAG and Ground Truth
tour.description: example_store.py is the ChromaDB few-shot store that the HARD-tier branch of the orchestrator queries — it embeds transcripts, retrieves the k most similar prior recordings from the corpus, and formats them as in-context examples in the extraction prompt. ground_truth.json is the gold-standard reference: 30 hand-labelled call recordings used both to seed the few-shot store and to score every metric in the next step. Together they close the loop between training data and runtime augmentation.

tour.title: Evaluation, Regression, and Escalation
tour.description: metrics.py is the core accuracy engine — it normalizes field values, compares extractions against ground_truth.json from Step 9, and produces per-field, overall, and per-recording breakdowns that drive every number in the README. benchmark.py segments those metrics by difficulty tier so you can see exactly where accuracy drops, regression.py snapshots a baseline and flags drops beyond a tolerance for CI, and escalation.py enqueues low-confidence extractions to a JSON queue for human review — the human-in-the-loop fallback for the recordings the model gets wrong.

tour.title: Observability Stack
tour.description: Five small modules form the production observability layer the README touts. logging.py configures structlog for JSON logs with helpers for common pipeline events; latency.py is an SLA-budget context manager that records per-node durations and flags p95 violations; cost_tracker.py accumulates token usage with model-specific pricing; alerts.py threshold-checks accuracy/cost/latency/escalation rate after every run; and prompt_registry.py SHA256-hashes every prompt and ties hashes to git commit and accuracy for exact reproducibility — the bridge between the next step's GEPA optimizer and the rest of the system.

tour.title: GEPA Optimization and Tests
tour.description: optimize.py is the GEPA prompt-optimization CLI: it treats the 5-slot prompt from Step 7 as a candidate, runs guided evolutionary search on a fixed train/val split using a reflection LM, and writes the winning prompt back as a new JSON file the registry from Step 11 can track. compare.py is the A/B harness that ingests outputs/results_*.json from multiple runs and prints per-field winners — this is how the README's 93% number was earned. test_extract.py rounds the tour out by exercising the V1 graph topology, retry loop, and CallerInfo schema, demonstrating the test-driven safety net around everything you have just toured.
